name: gh-flow
description: GitHub issue-driven workflow — PRD synthesis, plan-to-vertical-slice issues, bug triage with TDD fix plans, label-based state-machine triage, and conversational QA-to-issues. Requires GitHub remote and gh CLI. Adapted from mattpocock/skills.

interface.shortDescription: PRD/issues/triage workflow on GitHub. Requires gh CLI.
interface.longDescription: Five Matt Pocock skills bundled into a GitHub-issue-driven workflow: synthesize a PRD from current context (to-prd), break a plan into independently-grabbable vertical-slice issues (to-issues), triage a bug with codebase exploration and a TDD fix plan (triage-issue), label-based state-machine triage with agent-brief comments (github-triage), and a conversational QA session that files durable issues using the project's domain language (qa-to-issue, renamed from upstream qa to avoid clashing with the user's global /qa). All sub-skills check git remote -v before running and refuse if there is no GitHub remote.
defaultPrompt: Use $gh-flow to turn this conversation into a PRD and file it as a GitHub issue.
Use $gh-flow to break this plan into vertical-slice GitHub issues with dependencies marked.
Use $gh-flow to triage this bug — find the root cause and file an issue with a TDD fix plan.
